title: Syntax error when no parameter (empty) is provided
body:
Hi. I was testing an example from [here](https://github.com/laforest/FPGADesignElements) (Pipeline_FIFO_Buffer.v) where an empty parameter is assigned and Yosys fails. Here, a minimal example:

```
module issue (
    input  wire clock,
    output wire led
);

    Blink
    #(
        .INIT  ()
    )
    blink_inst
    (
        .clock (clock),
        .led   (led)
    );

endmodule
```

iverilog and Verilator say nothing, but with Yosys:

```
$ yosys -Q -p 'read_verilog issue.v'

-- Running command `read_verilog issue.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: issue.v
Parsing Verilog input from `issue.v' to AST representation.
issue.v:8: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected ')'
```

I first reported it [here](https://github.com/laforest/FPGADesignElements/issues/12), but the author tested against Quartus and Vivado and it worked as expected. Moreover, he said:
```
I dug through the Verilog-2001 (IEEE 1364-2001) standard, and in Appendix A.4.1 (Module instantiation), it explicitly shows that a named parameter assignment is of the form `. parameter_identifier ( [ expression ] )`, defining the expression as optional.
```

I know how to solve it in the Verilog to avoid the error. I am only reporting a possible parsing error.

Regards,
Rodrigo
code_blocks:
$ yosys -Q -p 'read_verilog issue.v'

-- Running command `read_verilog issue.v' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: issue.v
Parsing Verilog input from `issue.v' to AST representation.
issue.v:8: ERROR: syntax error, unexpected ')'

I dug through the Verilog-2001 (IEEE 1364-2001) standard, and in Appendix A.4.1 (Module instantiation), it explicitly shows that a named parameter assignment is of the form `. parameter_identifier ( [ expression ] )`, defining the expression as optional.
